question.label: Tradeoff narrative
question.name: What makes tradeoff conversations harder for you than the modeling itself?
question.description: Drills into the specific tradeoff-narrative gap she named before moving the arc forward.

answer: I want to lead one cross-team decision this cycle, end to end.

assessment.note: [SHALLOW] 'I want to lead one cross-team decision this cycle, end to end.' pivots to an aspiration and does not answer what makes tradeoff conversations harder; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] cross-team decision ownership surfaced but saved for next session.

new_queue.label: Signal choices
new_queue.name: What do you choose to make visible before the work is done?

new_queue.label: Next two weeks
new_queue.name: What would better stakeholder visibility look like in the next two weeks?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 7)

history.answer_excerpt: I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I tend to wait until analysis is complete before socializing direction.
That slows trust because stakeholders feel surprised late.
I need a tighter narrative on tradeoffs, not just stronger models.
I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I get work done, yet I am not consistently shaping decisions early.
Some feedback has been about being clearer with non-technical partners.
